titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the R. M. Koster Papers
titlestmt.author: by Annalise Hennessey

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday January 9, 2025 at 00:59

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection contains writings, newspaper clippings, papers relating to Panamanian politics, and ephemera documenting the career of American author R. M. Koster. Writings include typescript drafts, proofs, and galleys of Koster's published and unpublished works.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains writings, newspaper clippings, papers relating to Panamanian politics, and ephemera documenting the career of American author R. M. Koster. Writings include typescript drafts, proofs, and galleys of Koster's published and unpublished works. Notable works included are Carmichael's Dog , The Dissertation , Glass Mountain , Mandragon , The Prince , and In the Time of Tyrants: Panama: 1968-1990 , written with journalist Guillermo Sánchez Borbón. Panamanian political material includes memorandums, treaties, reports, and affidavits.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

bioghist.head: Koster, R. M. (1934-)
bioghist.p: Richard Morton Koster, an American author, journalist, and educator, was born on March 1, 1934 in Brooklyn, New York. In the late 1950s, he graduated from Yale University with a degree in English. Koster is best known for the Tinieblas Trilogy: The Prince (1972), a finalist for the 1973 National Book Award in Fiction; The Dissertation (1975); and Mandragon (1979). Other notable works include Carmichael's Dog (1992), The Glass Mountain (2001), and In the Time of the Tyrants: Panama: 1968-1990 (1991), a history of the military rule of Panama written with journalist Guillermo Sánchez Borbón. Koster moved to Panama in 1957. He taught English at the Panama Canal campus of Florida State University from 1964 to 2001 and has contributed articles to publications including Newsweek and The New York Times . Koster was also involved in American politics, serving on the Democratic National Committee from 1967 to 1996 and authoring presidential debate copy for Senator John Kerry in 2004.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: R. M. Koster Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The R. M. Koster papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Writings, 1950-2000. II. Panama Political Material, 1972-1989. III. Printed Material, 1975-1990.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of drafts and other materials related to books by Koster, including Carmichael's Dog , Glass Mountain , and Mandragon , and several published and unpublished shorter works.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in two subseries: Books and Shorter Works.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes typescript drafts, proofs, and galleys of books by Koster.

title.part: In the Time of the Tyrants: Panama, 1968-1990

did.unittitle: Proof, master set confirmation, first pass

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes typescript drafts of articles and essays by Koster.

did.unittitle: "The Smartest Woman in American Politics: A Profile of Anne Walker"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes writings and other papers by Koster related to Panamanian politics, including reports, treaties, memorandums, and affidavits.

did.unittitle: Panama and the murder that hunts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries includes one copy of Grupo de El Valle's Hacia Una Economia , newspaper clippings, and periodicals with works by Koster and others.

did.unittitle: El Valle, Grupo de, Hacia Una Economia
